title: cicuta sachalinensis
intro: Cicuta sachalinensis, also known as the Russian water hemlock, is a highly toxic and poisonous plant that belongs to the carrot family. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits associated with Cicuta sachalinensis are said to include reducing fever and alleviating pain, however it's crucial to note that its use can be life-threatening due to its toxicity. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in small amounts and under the guidance of a qualified practitioner, typically in the form of tinctures or infusions, although its use is extremely hazardous and not recommended for self-administration.
